image_prompt: Four waveform panels A-D in one row, each a sine wave on a fine-gridded box with the x-axis crossing at zero. All four share the SAME amplitude but have different periods: A shortest wavelength (~5 cycles), B ~3 cycles, C ~2 cycles, D longest wavelength (one cycle across the window). Answer = D, because a receding source stretches the wavelength (red-shift / Doppler effect).

render_data.title: Red-shift: which wave shows the source moving away?

options.note: Shorter wavelength than the stationary wave (blue-shift), not red-shift.
Slightly stretched, but not the most red-shifted option.
Stretched, but D shows a longer wavelength still.
Longest wavelength = lowest frequency: a receding source stretches the wave (Doppler red-shift).

render_data.task: A receding source stretches the wavelength (lower frequency). Pick the wave with the longest wavelength.

image_prompt: Hubble velocity-distance fan-shaped scatter through the origin. x-axis Distance / Mpc (0, 50, 100), y-axis Velocity / km s⁻¹ (0, 5000, 10⁴). Two populations with a key: grey 'galaxies observed today' forming a wide fan that widens with distance, and teal 'galaxies observed in 1930' clustered near the origin (small distances/velocities) giving the shallow 1930 best-fit and the 1.8 billion year age estimate. Part (d) asks for a best-fit line through the origin.

topic: Resistance vs length of a wire
image_prompt: SVG titled 'A graph of resistance against length for a conducting wire'. x-axis Length / m (0.00 to 1.20 step 0.20), y-axis Resistance / Ω (0.00 to 0.05 step 0.01). Five data points at (0.20,0.011), (0.40,0.020), (0.60,0.024), (0.80,0.034), (1.00,0.041), with a best-fit straight line of gradient ~0.035 Ω/m (variant value) and a NON-ZERO y-intercept of ~0.005 Ω (evidence of systematic error). Later parts compute the gradient and discuss the intercept.

render_data.prompt: Choose the circuit that correctly measures the current flowing through two LEDs connected in series.

options.label: Two LEDs in series with the battery, ammeter in series in the single loop
Two LEDs in parallel with each other, ammeter in series with the battery
Two LEDs in series with the battery, ammeter connected in parallel across one LED
Two LEDs in series with the battery, voltmeter (not ammeter) in series in the loop
